forecast-period.text: A cold front over the east of South Australia will move clear of the state by midday today. A second cold front will move across the west this afternoon reaching central and eastern parts early Friday morning. A cooler, fresh southerly flow following the second front will gradually ease as a high pressure system drifts in from the west for the weekend.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Gulf St Vincent
forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots during the afternoon and evening.
Westerly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots north of Port Vincent to Brighton early in the morning. Winds turning south to southwesterly during the day.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres around midday.
Southerly 15 to 20 knots turning southeasterly below 10 knots during the day.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the afternoon.
